mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below -0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (logistic regression coefficient insignificant at p > 0.10) over walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing limits, prompting inflation fears and central bank tightening expectations that compress equity multiples over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in late-cycle regimes yields positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, contradicting the tightening-fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate hike exceeding 0.25 percentage points in a single meeting abruptly tightens financial conditions, causing immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities and negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25pp in a single observation
failure_condition: Weekly S&P 500 returns following > 0.25pp rate hikes are positive in > 50% of historical instances, or event-study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer spending that lifts S&P 500 valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses significance in out-of-sample forecasting.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in real personal consumption expenditures during a post-shock recovery phase indicates demand fragility, forcing earnings downgrades and triggering negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines > 1.5% in post-shock regimes are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, invalidating the earnings downgrade channel.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indices reflect stronger manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, reducing input cost pressures and generating positive short-term equity momentum over one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO level changes show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all rolling 2-year evaluation windows.
